ddls:
CREATE TABLE "works" (
  "work_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each work */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the work */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the work was published or released */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of origin for the work */,
  "genre" TEXT /* Genre of the work (e.g., narrative, novel, film) */,
  "author" TEXT /* Author of the work */,
  "director" TEXT /* Director of the work */,
  "media_type" TEXT /* Type of media (e.g., novel, film, TV series) */,
  "publisher" TEXT /* Publisher or production company */,
  "isbn_or_identifier" TEXT /* ISBN for books, IMDB ID for films, etc. */,
  "plot_summary" TEXT /* A brief summary of the plot */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* A rating for the work (e.g., 1-10 scale) */,
  "review_count" INTEGER /* Number of reviews or ratings */,
  "tagline" TEXT /* A tagline or catchphrase for the work */,
  "language" TEXT /* The language in which the work was originally published or produced */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("work_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "technologies" (
  "tech_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each technology */,
  "tech_name" TEXT /* Name of the technology */,
  "tech_ref" TEXT /* Reference to the technology (e.g., Non-Visible Spectrum) */,
  "description" TEXT /* A detailed description of the technology */,
  "first_appearance" INTEGER /* The year the technology first appeared in science fiction */,
  "historical_influence" TEXT /* A brief note on the historical influence of the technology */,
  "real_world_equivalent" TEXT /* The real-world equivalent of the technology, if any */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tech_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "themes" (
  "theme_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each theme */,
  "theme_name" TEXT /* Name of the theme (e.g., AI, Space Exploration) */,
  "description" TEXT /* A detailed description of the theme */,
  "related_technologies" TEXT /* A list of related technologies */,
  "related_works" TEXT /* A list of related works */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("theme_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sentiments" (
  "sentiment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sentiment */,
  "sentiment_name" TEXT /* Name of the sentiment (e.g., Creepy, Futuristic) */,
  "description" TEXT /* A detailed description of the sentiment */,
  "related_works" TEXT /* A list of related works */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sentiment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "situations" (
  "situation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each situation */,
  "situation_name" TEXT /* Name of the situation (e.g., Space Exploration, AI Uprising) */,
  "description" TEXT /* A detailed description of the situation */,
  "related_technologies" TEXT /* A list of related technologies */,
  "related_characters" TEXT /* A list of related characters */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("situation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "characters" (
  "character_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each character */,
  "character_name" TEXT /* Name of the character (e.g., Dr. Heywood Floyd, HAL 9000) */,
  "description" TEXT /* A detailed description of the character */,
  "first_appearance" INTEGER /* The year the character first appeared in science fiction */,
  "traits" TEXT /* A list of character traits */,
  "related_technologies" TEXT /* A list of related technologies */,
  "related_works" TEXT /* A list of related works */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("character_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "work_technologies" (
  "work_id" INTEGER /* ID of the work */,
  "tech_id" INTEGER /* ID of the technology */,
  "tech_used" TEXT /* Description of how the technology is used in the work */,
  "importance" INTEGER /* A measure of the technology's importance in the work (e.g., 1-10 scale) */,
  "impact_on_narrative" TEXT /* A brief note on how the technology impacts the narrative */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("work_id", "tech_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "work_characters" (
  "work_id" INTEGER /* ID of the work */,
  "character_id" INTEGER /* ID of the character */,
  "role" TEXT /* The role of the character in the work (e.g., protagonist, antagonist) */,
  "importance" INTEGER /* A measure of the character's importance in the work (e.g., 1-10 scale) */,
  "character_traits" TEXT /* A list of character traits */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("work_id", "character_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "work_themes" (
  "work_id" INTEGER /* ID of the work */,
  "theme_id" INTEGER /* ID of the theme */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("work_id", "theme_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "work_sentiments" (
  "work_id" INTEGER /* ID of the work */,
  "sentiment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sentiment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("work_id", "sentiment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "work_situations" (
  "work_id" INTEGER /* ID of the work */,
  "situation_id" INTEGER /* ID of the situation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("work_id", "situation_id")
)
